senses.definition_dg: Lengo ambapo mutu analonda kuphiga au kufikira.

examples.en: He shot the arrow and hit the target.

senses.definition_en: to be foolish, to be weak minded
senses.definition_dg: Kukala mutu asiye na achili nyinji.

examples.en: Don't be foolish; use your intelligence.

senses.definition_en: , chara cha index finger, first or pointing finger
senses.definition_dg: Chala cha kpwandza cha kuonyeshera. Chala cha shahada.

examples.dg: Chara cha shahada ni chala cha kuonyesera.
examples.en: The index finger is the pointing finger.
examples.sw: Kidole cha shahada ni kidole cha kuonyesha.

senses.definition_dg: Mutu ariyeona mambo kpwa matso gakpwe na anaweza kugomba kuhusu go.

examples.en: One witness saw everything.

senses.definition_dg: Tatizo, hatari, au kukosa yakini.

examples.en: Don't have doubt; God will help you.

equivalents_en: Sable Antelope: large black antelope found in Shimba Hills National Park

senses.definition_en: Sable Antelope: large black antelope found in Shimba Hills National Park
senses.definition_dg: Aina ya nyama mkulu wa rangi nyiru anayepatikana Shimba Hills.

examples.dg: Shambi ni nyama mkulu anayeishi Shimba Hills.
examples.en: The Sable Antelope is a large animal that lives in Shimba Hills.
examples.sw: Shambi ni mnyama mkubwa anayeishi Shimba Hills.

senses.definition_dg: Tendo ra kushambuliya mutu au phatu.

examples.dg: Shambulizi ra maadui rikahendesa atu ahambire.
examples.en: The enemy attack made people flee.
examples.sw: Shambulizi la maadui liliwafanya watu wakimbie.

senses.definition_dg: Nguo nyere ya kufunikira maiti.

examples.en: The body was wrapped in a white shroud.

senses.definition_dg: Mtsanga mwororo wa kuhereza.

examples.dg: Phatu hipha phana shangasheshe; usinyendeke kpwa haraka.
examples.en: This place has sandy soil; don't walk quickly.
examples.sw: Mahali hapa pana mchanga wa tifutifu; usitembee haraka.

senses.definition_dg: Dada wa baba yako.

examples.dg: Shangazi wangu anaishi mudzi wa jirani.
examples.en: My paternal aunt lives in the neighbouring village.
examples.sw: Shangazi yangu anaishi kijiji cha jirani.

senses.definition_dg: Furaha kulu ya sherehe.

examples.dg: Wakati wa arusi kuna shangbwe nyinji.
examples.en: During a wedding there is much rejoicing.
examples.sw: Wakati wa arusi kuna shangwe nyingi.

equivalents_en: type of disease (especially affecting pregnant women) involving a genital discharge which is identified and treated by traditional healers
equivalents_sw: aina ya ugonjwa unaosumbua wanawake enye mimba

senses.definition_en: type of disease (especially affecting pregnant women) involving a genital discharge which is identified and treated by traditional healers
senses.definition_sw: aina ya ugonjwa unaosumbua wanawake enye mimba
senses.definition_dg: Ugonjwa unaogbwira achetu enye mimba, unaotambuliwa na kuphozwa ni waganga wa chizamani.

examples.dg: Mchetu iye ana shango; amphiye mganga.
examples.en: That woman has the shango disease; take her to the healer.
examples.sw: Mwanamke yule ana shango; mpeleke kwa mganga.

examples.en: Chishindilia put on a loosely fitting shirt

senses.definition_dg: Uamuzi au ushauri unaopewa mutu.

examples.dg: Shauri ra mzee ni muhimu; risikire.
examples.en: The elder's advice is important; listen to it.
examples.sw: Shauri la mzee ni muhimu; lisikilize.

senses.definition_dg: Kupha mutu ushauri kuhusu jambo.

examples.en: Advise me on what to do about this problem.

equivalents_en: relation by marriage of same generation
equivalents_sw: indirect way of referring to one's wife; shemeji

senses.definition_en: relation by marriage of same generation, in-law, i.e. wife's (or husband's) brother or sister
senses.definition_sw: indirect way of referring to one's wife; shemeji
senses.definition_dg: Ndugu wa mkpwaziye au mkpwazako — dada au kaka wa mutu uriyemuhala.

examples.dg: Shemegi wangu ni mutu mnono sana.
examples.en: My in-law is a very good person.
examples.sw: Shemeji wangu ni mtu mzuri sana.

senses.definition_dg: Angaliya sherehe.

examples.dg: Sheree ni neno ranjina ra sherehe.
examples.en: Sheree is another word for sherehe (celebration).
examples.sw: Sheree ni neno lingine la sherehe.

senses.definition_dg: Mkutano wa furaha wa atu kpwa sababu ya jambo nono.

examples.dg: Sherehe ya arusi ikakala nono sana.
examples.en: The wedding celebration was very wonderful.
examples.sw: Sherehe ya arusi ilikuwa nzuri sana.

senses.definition_dg: Kuhenda sherehe kpwa furaha.

examples.dg: Atu anasherekea ushindi wa timu yao.
examples.en: People are celebrating their team's victory.
examples.sw: Watu wanasherehekea ushindi wa timu yao.

equivalents_en: spirit which lives in forests or near large trees (especially baobabs)
equivalents_sw: they may occasionally be seen but do not possess people in

senses.definition_en: spirit which lives in forests or near large trees (especially baobabs)
senses.definition_sw: they may occasionally be seen but do not possess people in
senses.definition_dg: Pepo mbii ambayo inaishi tsakani au phephi na mihi mikulu, hasa mibuyu.

examples.dg: Shetani anakala tsakani phephi na mihi mikulu.
examples.en: The spirit dwells in forests near large trees.
examples.sw: Shetani anakaa porini karibu na miti mikubwa.

senses.definition_en: to be full of food, to be sated
senses.definition_dg: Kukala umemala kurya na tumbo rikajaa.

examples.en: I am full; I don't want more food.

senses.definition_dg: Angaliya shuwari.

examples.dg: Shibe ni neno ranjina ra shuwari.
examples.en: Shibe is another word for shuwari.
examples.sw: Shibe ni neno lingine la shuwari.

senses.definition_dg: Hali ya kukala ukashiba.

examples.en: She doesn't want to eat because she is full

senses.definition_dg: Tatizo au hali ngumu ya maisha.

examples.en: He has many life problems.

equivalents_en: a common greeting used by young people to elders

senses.definition_en: a common greeting used by young people to elders, formerly
senses.definition_dg: Salamu ya hishima ambayo avulana na achetu analumusa azee.

examples.en: The child said 'Shikamoo' to the elder.

senses.definition_dg: Hela ya tsi ya Kenya na Tanzania.

examples.dg: Bei ya muhogo ni shilingi mia mbiri.
examples.en: The price of cassava is two hundred shillings.
examples.sw: Bei ya muhogo ni shilingi mia mbili.

senses.definition_en: to win, to defeat, to beat someone
senses.definition_dg: Kuphaha ushindi kpwa mchezo au malumbano.

examples.en: Our team won the game.

senses.definition_dg: Dzino ra nyuma ra kusagia chakurya.

examples.en: My molar hurts a lot.

senses.definition_dg: Kutsukuza nyuni au nyama ili akimbire.

examples.en: The child is scaring birds in the farm.

senses.definition_en: to share in, to participate, to take part in
senses.definition_dg: Kuhenda phamwenga na atu anjina.

examples.en: Jealousy is a sore, if you share in it you will waste away
examples.sw: Wivu ni kidonda, ukishiriki utapona — methali ya Kidigo.

senses.definition_dg: Maneno ga kumheya mutu kpwa kosa arirohenda.

examples.en: The accusation against the thief was taken to court.

senses.definition_dg: Kupeleka mutu kotini kpwa kosa arirohenda.

examples.en: He prosecuted his neighbour in court.

senses.definition_dg: Kudziikira dzulu, kudziona bora kuriko anjina.

examples.dg: Usihende shobo; unyenyekevu ni mnono zaidi.
examples.en: Don't be proud; humility is better.

senses.definition_dg: Mulume ambaye ana tabia za kichetu.

examples.dg: Shoga ni mutu ambaye ana tabia za kichetu.
examples.en: A shoga is a man with effeminate manners.
examples.sw: Shoga ni mtu aliye na tabia za kike.

senses.definition_en: to be forced to do something
senses.definition_dg: Kulazimishwa kuhenda chitu usichondzo.

examples.en: He was forced to do that work.

senses.definition_dg: Kuunganisha nguwo kpwa sindano na uzi.

examples.en: Mother is sewing her child's clothes.
examples.sw: Mama anashona nguo ya mtoto wake.

senses.definition_dg: Chundu ya mavi ya nyama au kuku.

examples.dg: Hala shonde ya ng'ombe uike mundani dza mbolea.
examples.en: Take the cow dung and put it in the farm as fertilizer.
examples.sw: Chukua shonde la ng'ombe uweke shambani kama mbolea.

equivalents_en: evil spirit known especially in Tanzania

senses.definition_en: evil spirit known especially in Tanzania
senses.definition_dg: Pepo mbii ambayo inamanyikana hasa Tanzania.

examples.dg: Shubiani ni pepo wa hatari sana.
examples.en: Shubiani is a very dangerous evil spirit.
examples.sw: Shubiani ni pepo wa hatari sana.

senses.definition_dg: Chundu ya mavi. Sawa na shonde.

examples.dg: Shudu ni neno ranjina ra shonde.
examples.en: Shudu is another word for shonde (dung heap).
examples.sw: Shudu ni neno lingine la shonde.

senses.definition_dg: Kutoa hewa kula tumboni kpwa njia ya nyuma na sauti.

examples.en: Don't fart loudly in front of people.

senses.definition_dg: Mududu kukugonga na kukutiya sumu ya kuuma.

examples.en: A bee stung me

senses.definition_dg: Kugomba maneno ga kpweli kuhusu chitu urichokiona.

examples.en: He testified before the judge.

senses.definition_dg: Kazi au mambo yanayohendwa.

examples.en: Today's activities are many.

senses.definition_en: sheet, piece of cloth wrapped around the waist
senses.definition_dg: Nguo ya kufunikira mwiri au ya kuika chitandani.

examples.en: He wrapped a white sheet around himself and slept.

senses.definition_dg: Maneno ga kumshukuru mutu kpwa chitu chinono arichokuhendere.

examples.en: Many thanks for your help.

senses.definition_dg: Kuonyesha shukurani kpwa mutu au kpwa Mlungu.

examples.en: Thank God for his blessings.

senses.definition_dg: Phatu ambapo anache anaphiya kusoma na kujifundza.

examples.en: Children go to school in the morning.

senses.definition_en: to force someone to do something
senses.definition_dg: Kumhenda mutu ahende chitu asichoronda kpwa nguvu.

examples.en: Don't force a child who has refused.

senses.definition_dg: Nguo au chitu kupata doa au alama ya uchafu.

examples.en: My clothes got stained with paint.
examples.sw: Nguo yangu ilipata doa la rangi.
